title: BLEU: N-gram Precision
concept: BLEU (Bilingual Evaluation Understudy) measures the precision of n-grams in the model's output compared to multiple human reference translations.

exercises.prompt: What is the BLEU metric primarily used for in AI?

options.text: Generating realistic images of blue skies

exercises.explain: BLEU stands for Bilingual Evaluation Understudy. It is a widely used metric for evaluating the quality of machine-translated text.

cards.title: Grading the Robots
cards.body: How do we know if a computer's translation of a book or website is actually good without hiring thousands of humans to read it? We use a smart math shortcut called BLEU to automatically grade the translation in seconds!

cards.title: What is BLEU?
cards.body: BLEU (Bilingual Evaluation Understudy) is a tool that evaluates machine-generated translations. It does this by checking how many groups of words (n-grams) in the computer's translation also appear in high-quality human reference translations.

cards.title: The Cake Recipe Match
cards.body: Imagine you are baking a cake using a recipe book. The BLEU score is like a judge who checks your cake by counting how many specific ingredients (like 'dark chocolate' or 'fresh strawberries') you used that exactly match the recipe's ingredient list. The more correct combinations you match, the higher your score!

cards.title: BLEU in Action
cards.body: If a machine translates a sentence as 'The cat sat', and the human reference says 'The cat sat on the mat', BLEU checks the matching words. The individual words 'The', 'cat', and 'sat' match, as do the pairs 'The cat' and 'cat sat'. Because of these matches, the machine gets a high precision score!

cards.title: Is 100% Necessary?
cards.body: People think a BLEU score of 100% is required for a translation to be readable. In reality, even human translators rarely get a perfect 100% because there are many different, correct ways to translate the same sentence! A score of 30% to 40% is often considered very good.

cards.title: Comparing Words
cards.body: BLEU compares the words produced by the machine against trusted human translations to check for accuracy.

left.text: 'The dog sleeps under the warm sun'

exercises.prompt: What does the BLEU metric primarily measure to evaluate a translation model?

options.text: The speed at which the model generates text
The precision of n-grams compared to human references
The number of spelling errors in the dictionary
The size of the computer hard drive

exercises.explain: BLEU calculates precision by checking the overlap of word groups (n-grams) between the machine translation and human reference translations.

exercises.prompt: True or False: BLEU evaluates translations by comparing them to random computer-generated text.
exercises.explain: BLEU compares the machine's output to human-written reference translations, not to random computer-generated text.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of how BLEU measures translation quality.
exercises.sentence: BLEU works by measuring the precision of overlapping [blank] between the computer output and human references.
exercises.explain: BLEU compares groups of words, known as n-grams, between the computer's translation and the human references to calculate precision.

exercises.prompt: Three AI translation models discuss how they calculate or use BLEU scores. Spot the AI that does NOT understand how BLEU works.

options.text: AI Model A: 'My translation matches 80% of the 2-word phrases in the human translation, so my BLEU score is high!'
AI Model B: 'I calculated my BLEU score by running a Python script to see if the code has syntax errors.'
AI Model C: 'Even though my translation is slightly different from the reference, I still got a decent BLEU score.'

exercises.explain: AI Model B is incorrect because BLEU evaluates translation quality by comparing word matches, not by checking if code runs successfully.

exercises.prompt: You are building a translation app and want to use BLEU to test your model. How does BLEU actually calculate its score?

options.text: It counts how many n-grams in the machine translation match the human reference translations.
It reads the translation aloud to check if the pronunciation sounds natural.
It translates the text into ten different languages and averages the length.
It checks if the computer code runs without crashing or freezing.

exercises.explain: BLEU evaluates machine translation by checking how many of its n-grams match the human reference translations. However, it cannot easily measure if a sentence is grammatically perfect or captures deep artistic meaning; it simply counts overlapping word groups.
